title: Docs split — DELETE docs/schema.md, CREATE docs/data-schema.md + docs/mcp-contracts.md
description: rc.2-prep shipped a single `docs/schema.md` covering frontmatter + event types. Per Q7 grill-locked decision, split into TWO files for clearer separation of concerns: (1) `docs/data-schema.md` — DATA shape (what's persisted): frontmatter 7 fields, 24 event types (13 base + 11 newly registered), stable_id format, dual counters envelope, meta.revision formula, naming guidelines, language policy, dual-meta-file protocol; (2) `docs/mcp-contracts.md` — API contracts (what's invoked): 4 MCP tool signatures with input/output schemas derived from Zod, 'thin MCP, thick Skill' design principle, Skill↔MCP responsibility matrix. Both files cross-reference each other. DELETE old `docs/schema.md` (single source pre-split — replaced).

files.change: Delete the old combined schema doc shipped in rc.2-prep — replaced by docs/data-schema.md + docs/mcp-contracts.md

Create new data-schema doc covering: (a) frontmatter 7 fields (id, type, maturity, layer, layer_reason, created_at, tags); (b) 24 event types organized into sections (13 base + 11 newly registered for rc.2/3/4); (c) stable_id format `K[PT]-(D|P|G|M|PR)-NNNN` decomposition; (d) dual counters envelope — KT in repo `.fabric/agents.meta.json`, KP in `~/.fabric/agents.meta.json`, independent monotonic; (e) meta.revision formula = sha256(sorted(stable_id + ':' + frontmatter_hash)); (f) naming guidelines — 5 rules (kebab-case, 2-5 words, 20-40 chars, semantic core, unique within type+layer); (g) language policy — M3 style detection logic + match-existing fallback; (h) dual-meta-file protocol — independent counters, MCP merges both at read time. Cross-link to mcp-contracts.md for API surface.

Create new MCP-contracts doc covering: (a) 4 MCP tool contracts with input/output Zod-derived schemas — `fab_plan_context` (existing, with new layer_filter field), `fab_get_rule_sections` (existing, with new redirect_to error), `fab_extract_knowledge` (rc.2 — semi-thick input), `fab_review` (rc.3 — 6-action discriminated union); (b) 'thin MCP, thick Skill' design principle explanation; (c) Skill↔MCP responsibility matrix table — which decisions live in LLM end (Skill) vs deterministic end (MCP). Cross-link to data-schema.md for data shapes.

implementation: Read existing docs/schema.md to absorb existing content + tone — ensure nothing of value is lost in the split
Draft data-schema.md outline: 8 sections matching the (a)..(h) breakdown in files[].change
Section (a) Frontmatter: derive 7 fields from packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts; document each field's type/purpose/example

Section (b) Event Types: list all 24 (13 from rc.2-prep + 11 from TASK-004); group by lifecycle phase (proposed → review → promoted/rejected/deferred → demoted/archived); for each event give name + minimal payload skeleton

Section (c) stable_id: explain `K[PT]-(D|P|G|M|PR)-NNNN` — K=knowledge prefix, P/T=personal/team layer, D/P/G/M/PR=type code (decisions/pitfalls/guidelines/models/processes), NNNN=monotonic counter zero-padded; show example KT-DEC-0001

Section (d) Counters envelope: explain dual-meta-file protocol — KT counters in repo's .fabric/agents.meta.json, KP counters in ~/.fabric/agents.meta.json; independent monotonic; never reset; layer-flip does NOT decrement old layer's counter
Section (e) meta.revision: define formula `sha256(sorted(stable_id + ':' + frontmatter_hash))`; explain when recomputed (any frontmatter change, any add/remove)
Section (f) Naming: 5 rules — kebab-case slugs, 2-5 words, 20-40 chars total, semantic core (verb-or-noun phrase), unique within (type, layer)

Section (g) Language policy: M3 style = EN headings + zh-CN/EN body + EN tech terms preserved; match-existing detection scans repo's existing prose; default 'en' on empty repo; per-config override via knowledge_language field
Section (h) Dual-meta-file protocol: KT meta in repo, KP meta in ~; MCP reads both at startup and merges into single in-memory view; writes go to layer-appropriate file
Draft mcp-contracts.md outline: 3 sections — (a) 4 tool contracts, (b) thin/thick design principle, (c) responsibility matrix table
Section (a) — for each of 4 tools: heading + input/output schema (copied or derived from Zod definition), description, error shapes, idempotency notes
Section (b) — explain why MCP is thin (deterministic CRUD) and Skill is thick (LLM judgment carries context); cite Q7 decision
Section (c) — markdown table with columns [Decision Type, Lives In, Why]; rows = e.g. 'Tag inference' → Skill, 'Counter increment' → MCP, 'Idempotency dedup' → MCP, 'Slug naming choice' → Skill, etc.

Cross-references: in data-schema.md, link to mcp-contracts.md from the event-types section (events emitted by which tool); in mcp-contracts.md, link to data-schema.md from input/output schemas (referencing field semantics)
Length check: each new doc ≥ 150 lines
Run `git status docs/` — confirm: deleted schema.md + 2 untracked new files
Eyeball both files for tone/clarity (Gemini diff review will catch issues)

reference.pattern: Documentation split for separation of concerns — data shape vs API contract
reference.examples: Follow tone of existing docs/schema.md (concise, contract-style, no marketing); use markdown tables for matrices; code-fence Zod schemas inline

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: README rewrite (rc.4); marketing copy; docs/knowledge-types.md, docs/initialization.md, docs/roadmap.md (all rc.4); auto-generation of docs from Zod (rc.4 candidate). Strictly: 1 delete + 2 creates with cross-references.

rationale.chosen_approach: Split docs/schema.md into 2 files (data-schema.md + mcp-contracts.md) for clearer separation of data shape vs API surface; both reference each other to preserve discoverability
alternatives_considered: Three-way split (data + api + design — rejected, design fits inside mcp-contracts.md)
Defer split to rc.4 (rejected — schemas land in TASK-002/003/004 NOW, docs should reflect current state)
decision_factors: Q7 grill decision locked the split
Data shape and API surface have different audiences (data = anyone reading entries on disk; API = anyone integrating with MCP)
rc.2/3 implementation tasks need clear API contract reference — single combined doc dilutes signal
rationale.tradeoffs: Two files instead of one — slight discovery cost mitigated by mutual cross-references; combined word count likely > old single doc — accepted for clarity

manual_checks: Eyeball data-schema.md — 8 sections present, each with concrete examples not just prose
Eyeball mcp-contracts.md — 4 tool contracts with input/output Zod-derived schemas, responsibility matrix table renders cleanly
Cross-references work: clicking link in data-schema → mcp-contracts and vice versa
No remaining mentions of removed docs/schema.md in repo (`grep -r 'docs/schema\.md' .` empty)
success_metrics: Both new docs exist with ≥ 150 lines each
All 4 MCP tools and 24 event types documented
Reads cleanly to a human reviewer (Gemini diff review)
integration: Zod schema field names in docs match actual schema source (verify by spot-grep against packages/shared/src/schemas/)
Event type names in docs match TASK-004's added z.literal variants

risks.description: Docs may diverge from code reality if rc.2/3 implementation reveals schema gaps that change Zod definitions
risks.mitigation: Add a note to rc.2/3 task lists to update docs/data-schema.md and docs/mcp-contracts.md when schemas change; consider auto-gen from Zod in rc.4
risks.fallback: Treat divergence as a hotfix in rc.2/3.fix.N or update inline during the relevant rc

risks.description: Cross-references between the 2 new docs break if file paths/heading anchors change
risks.mitigation: Use stable section headings; verify links manually in the diff review
risks.fallback: Fix broken links as a follow-up commit if discovered
